title: Japanese Text Processing Tools
text: audubon Japanese text processing tools : hiraganization, katakanization romanization hakatashi/japanese.js segmentation phrase google/budoux ‘TinySegmenter.js’ text normalization based rules ‘Sudachi’ morphological analyzer ‘NEologd’ (Neologism dictionary ‘MeCab’)

title: Japanese Text Processing Tools
text: strj_normalize normalizes text following rule based NEologd style. strj_rewrite_as_def R port SudachiCharNormalizer typically normalizes characters following ’*.def’ file. audubon package contains several ’*.def’ files, can use write ‘rewrite.def’ file follows. feature powerful stringi::stri_trans_* allows users control characters normalized. instance, function can used convert kyuji-tai characters shinji-tai characters.
code: strj_normalize("――南アルプスの　天然水-　Ｓｐａｒｋｉｎｇ*　Ｌｅｍｏｎ+　レモン一絞り") #> [1] "ー南アルプスの天然水-Sparking* Lemon+レモン一絞り" # single characters will **never** be normalized. … # if two characters are separated with a tab, # left side forms are always rewritten to right side forms # before normalized. 斎   斉 齋   斉 齊   斉 # supports rewriting a single character to a single character, # i.e., this cannot work. ｱｯ  ア stringi::stri_trans_nfkc("Ⅹⅳ") #> [1] "Xiv" strj_rewrite_as_def("Ⅹⅳ") #> [1] "Ⅹⅳ" strj_rewrite_as_def("惡と假面のルール", read_rewrite_def(system.file("def/kyuji.def", package = "audubon"))) #> [1] "悪と仮面のルール"

title: Japanese Text Processing Tools
text: audubon provides small helper functions designed work ggplot2 labellers, making easier format Japanese text dates plots. labellers intended cases default wrapping formatting insufficient Japanese text, ICU-based locale handling can leveraged without manual preprocessing.

previous_headings: Usage > Japanese label helpers for ggplot2
title: Japanese Text Processing Tools
text: label_wrap_jp() label_wrap_jp_gen() wrap labels natural Japanese phrase boundaries rather fixed character widths. useful discrete scales long Japanese labels, titles, phrases, excerpts.

previous_headings: Usage > Japanese label helpers for ggplot2
title: Japanese Text Processing Tools
text: label_date_jp() label_date_jp_gen() format date labels using Japanese calendar system, including era-based representations. can used date datetime scales produce locale-aware Japanese date labels without manual formatting.
code: date_range <- function(start, days) {   start <- as.POSIXct(start)   c(start, start + days * 24 * 60 * 60) } two_months <- date_range("2025-12-31", 60) scales::demo_datetime(two_months, labels = label_date_jp_gen()) #> scale_x_datetime(labels = label_date_jp_gen())

title: Japanese Text Processing Tools

what: audubon: Japanese Text Processing Tools — audubon-package
title: audubon: Japanese Text Processing Tools — audubon-package
text: collection Japanese text processing tools filling Japanese iteration marks, Japanese character type conversions, segmentation phrase, text normalization based rules 'Sudachi' morphological analyzer 'NEologd' (Neologism dictionary 'MeCab'). features specific Japanese implemented 'ICU' (International Components Unicode).

title: audubon: Japanese Text Processing Tools — audubon-package
text: Maintainer: Akiru Kato paithiov909@gmail.com contributors: Koki Takahashi (Author japanese.js) [copyright holder] Shuhei Iitsuka (Author budoux) [copyright holder] Taku Kudo (Author TinySegmenter) [copyright holder]

title: Default Japanese date format — default_format
text: Returns default date format string used Japanese calendar date parsing formatting. helper function exists provide UTF-8 encoded format string without embedding non-ASCII characters directly function defaults.

title: Default Japanese date format — default_format
text: character string representing Japanese calendar date format.

what: Japanese date labeller for ggplot2 — label_date_jp
title: Japanese date labeller for ggplot2 — label_date_jp
text: Formats date labels using Japanese calendar system returns labels suitable use ggplot2 scales.

title: Japanese date labeller for ggplot2 — label_date_jp
code: label_date_jp(labels, format = default_format(), tz = NULL)  label_date_jp_gen(format = default_format(), tz = NULL)

title: Japanese date labeller for ggplot2 — label_date_jp
text: labels vector values coercible Date objects. format date-time format string following ICU conventions. tz time zone used coercing values Date objects.

title: Japanese date labeller for ggplot2 — label_date_jp
text: label_date_jp() returns character vector formatted date labels. label_date_jp_gen() returns labeller function use ggplot2 scales.

title: Japanese date labeller for ggplot2 — label_date_jp
text: labeller formats dates according locale-aware Japanese calendar, allowing era-based representations Reiwa Heisei. output intended discrete continuous date scales ggplot2.

title: Japanese date labeller for ggplot2 — label_date_jp
code: date_range <- function(start, days) {   start <- as.POSIXct(start)   c(start, start + days * 24 * 60 * 60) } two_months <- date_range("2025-12-31", 60)  label_date_jp(two_months) #> [1] "令和7年12月31日" "令和8年3月1日"   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (requireNamespace("scales", quietly = TRUE)) {   scales::demo_datetime(two_months, labels = label_date_jp_gen()) } } # }

what: Japanese word-wrapping labeller for ggplot2 — label_wrap_jp
title: Japanese word-wrapping labeller for ggplot2 — label_wrap_jp
text: Wraps character strings using Japanese phrase boundaries returns labels suitable use ggplot2 scales.

title: Japanese word-wrapping labeller for ggplot2 — label_wrap_jp
code: label_wrap_jp(labels, wrap = 16, width = 50, collapse = "\n")  label_wrap_jp_gen(wrap = 16, width = 50, collapse = "\n")

title: Japanese word-wrapping labeller for ggplot2 — label_wrap_jp
text: labels character vector labels wrap. wrap integer giving target number characters per line. width integer giving maximum total width wrapped label. collapse character string used join wrapped lines.

title: Japanese word-wrapping labeller for ggplot2 — label_wrap_jp
text: label_wrap_jp() returns character vector wrapped labels. label_wrap_jp_gen() returns labeller function use ggplot2 scales.

title: Japanese word-wrapping labeller for ggplot2 — label_wrap_jp
text: labeller uses ICU-based Japanese phrase boundary detection insert line breaks natural word boundaries. Long labels can truncated fixed display width ellipsis.

title: Japanese word-wrapping labeller for ggplot2 — label_wrap_jp
code: label_wrap_jp(polano[4:6], width = 32) #> [1] "宮沢賢治訳述"                                                  #> [2] "そのころわたくしは、モリーオ市の\n博物局に勤めて居りました"    #> [3] "十八等官でしたから役所のなかでも、\nずうっと下の方でしたし..." if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (requireNamespace("scales", quietly = TRUE)) {   scales::demo_discrete(polano[4:6], labels = label_wrap_jp_gen()) } } # }

title: Whole text of 'Porano no Hiroba' written by Miyazawa Kenji from Aozora Bunko — polano
text: https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000081/files/1935_ruby_19924.zip

title: Whole text of 'Porano no Hiroba' written by Miyazawa Kenji from Aozora Bunko — polano
text: dataset containing text Miyazawa Kenji's novel "Porano Hiroba" published 1934, year Kenji's death. Copyright work expired since 70 years passed author's death. UTF-8 plain text sourced https://www.aozora.gr.jp/cards/000081/card1935.html cleaned meta data.

title: Whole text of 'Porano no Hiroba' written by Miyazawa Kenji from Aozora Bunko — polano
code: head(polano) #> [1] "ポラーノの広場"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           #> [2] "宮沢賢治"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 #> [3] "前十七等官レオーノ・キュースト誌"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         #> [4] "宮沢賢治訳述"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             #> [5] "そのころわたくしは、モリーオ市の博物局に勤めて居りました。"                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               #> [6] "十八等官でしたから役所のなかでも、ずうっと下の方でしたし俸給もほんのわずかでしたが、受持ちが標本の採集や整理で生れ付き好きなことでしたから、わたくしは毎日ずいぶん愉快にはたらきました。殊にそのころ、モリーオ市では競馬場を植物園に拵え直すというので、その景色のいいまわりにアカシヤを植え込んだ広い地面が、切符売場や信号所の建物のついたまま、わたくしどもの役所の方へまわって来たものですから、わたくしはすぐ宿直という名前で月賦で買った小さな蓄音器と二十枚ばかりのレコードをもって、その番小屋にひとり住むことになりました。わたくしはそこの馬を置く場所に板で小さなしきいをつけて一疋の山羊を飼いました。毎朝その乳をしぼってつめたいパンをひたしてたべ、それから黒い革のかばんへすこしの書類や雑誌を入れ、靴もきれいにみがき、並木のポプラの影法師を大股にわたって市の役所へ出て行くのでした。"

title: Read rewrite definition file — read_rewrite_def
text: Reads rewrite definition file used Japanese text normalization. function parses tab-delimited definition file returns list rewrite rules ignored characters suitable use strj_rewrite_as_def().

title: Read rewrite definition file — read_rewrite_def
text: def_path file path rewrite definition file.

title: Read rewrite definition file — read_rewrite_def
text: list containing rewrite rules ignored characters.

title: Convert Japanese kana characters — strj-hira-kana
text: Converts Japanese text hiragana katakana representations. functions transform kana characters preserving non-kana characters. conversion based JavaScript implementation handles certain historical contracted kana forms covered standard Unicode transliteration alone.

title: Convert Japanese kana characters — strj-hira-kana
text: text character vector containing Japanese text.

title: Convert Japanese kana characters — strj-hira-kana
text: character vector kana characters converted target script.

title: Convert Japanese kana characters — strj-hira-kana
text: conversion behavior largely compatible ICU-based transliteration, additional support selected combined historical kana characters.

title: Convert Japanese kana characters — strj-hira-kana
code: strj_hiraganize(   c(     paste0(       "\u3042\u306e\u30a4\u30fc\u30cf\u30c8",       "\u30fc\u30f4\u30a9\u306e\u3059\u304d",       "\u3068\u304a\u3063\u305f\u98a8"     ),     "\u677f\u57a3\u6b7b\u30b9\U0002a708"   ) ) #> [1] "あのいーはとーゔぉのすきとおった風" "板垣死すとも"                       strj_katakanize(   c(     paste0(       "\u3042\u306e\u30a4\u30fc\u30cf\u30c8",       "\u30fc\u30f4\u30a9\u306e\u3059\u304d",       "\u3068\u304a\u3063\u305f\u98a8"     ),     "\u672c\u65e5\u309f\u304b\u304d\u6c37\u89e3\u7981"   ) ) #> [1] "アノイーハトーヴォノスキトオッタ風" "本日ヨリカキ氷解禁"

title: Fill Japanese iteration marks — strj_fill_iter_mark
text: Replaces Japanese iteration marks character strings corresponding repeated characters. function scans input string expands iteration marks odoriji inferring characters repeated surrounding context. implementation heuristic intended practical text normalization rather complete linguistic accuracy.

title: Fill Japanese iteration marks — strj_fill_iter_mark
text: text character vector containing Japanese text.

title: Fill Japanese iteration marks — strj_fill_iter_mark
text: character vector iteration marks replaced inferred repeated characters.

title: Fill Japanese iteration marks — strj_fill_iter_mark
text: restoration based local character context may incomplete iteration marks refer longer complex spans.

what: Convert text following the rules of 'NEologd' — strj_normalize
title: Convert text following the rules of 'NEologd' — strj_normalize
text: Converts characters normalized style following rule recommended Neologism dictionary 'MeCab'.

title: Convert text following the rules of 'NEologd' — strj_normalize
Convert text following the rules of 'NEologd' — strj_normalize
text: text character vector containing Japanese text.

title: Convert text following the rules of 'NEologd' — strj_normalize
text: character vector normalized text.

title: Convert text following the rules of 'NEologd' — strj_normalize
code: strj_normalize(   paste0(     "\u2015\u2015\u5357\u30a2\u30eb\u30d7\u30b9",     "\u306e\u3000\u5929\u7136\u6c34-\u3000\uff33",     "\uff50\uff41\uff52\uff4b\uff49\uff4e\uff47*",     "\u3000\uff2c\uff45\uff4d\uff4f\uff4e+",     "\u3000\u30ec\u30e2\u30f3\u4e00\u7d5e\u308a"   ) ) #> [1] "ー南アルプスの天然水-Sparking* Lemon+レモン一絞り"

title: Parse Japanese calendar dates — strj_parse_date
text: Parses Japanese calendar date strings POSIXct objects. function parses date strings formatted Japanese calendar system converts POSIXct values using locale-aware ICU parsing.

title: Parse Japanese calendar dates — strj_parse_date
text: date character vector containing Japanese calendar date strings. format date-time format string following ICU conventions. tz time zone used resulting POSIXct values.

title: Parse Japanese calendar dates — strj_parse_date
text: POSIXct vector representing parsed dates.

title: Parse Japanese calendar dates — strj_parse_date
text: Partial date specifications interpreted according ICU parsing rules may result completion current date time components.

what: Rewrite Japanese text using normalization rules — strj_rewrite_as_def
title: Rewrite Japanese text using normalization rules — strj_rewrite_as_def
text: Rewrites Japanese text according set normalization rules modeled Sudachi dictionary definitions.

title: Rewrite Japanese text using normalization rules — strj_rewrite_as_def
Rewrite Japanese text using normalization rules — strj_rewrite_as_def
text: text character vector containing Japanese text. rewrite definition object returned read_rewrite_def().

title: Rewrite Japanese text using normalization rules — strj_rewrite_as_def
text: character vector rewritten normalized text.

title: Rewrite Japanese text using normalization rules — strj_rewrite_as_def
text: function applies character-level rewrite rules normalize variant forms optionally ignoring specified characters. implementation simplified heuristic adaptation Sudachi-style normalization. rewrite process based fixed replacement rules aim fully reproduce Sudachi's normalization behavior.

title: Rewrite Japanese text using normalization rules — strj_rewrite_as_def
code: strj_rewrite_as_def(   paste0(     "\u2015\u2015\u5357\u30a2\u30eb",     "\u30d7\u30b9\u306e\u3000\u5929",     "\u7136\u6c34-\u3000\uff33\uff50",     "\uff41\uff52\uff4b\uff49\uff4e\uff47*",     "\u3000\uff2c\uff45\uff4d\uff4f\uff4e+",     "\u3000\u30ec\u30e2\u30f3\u4e00\u7d5e\u308a"   ) ) #> [1] "――南アルプスの 天然水- Sparking* Lemon+ レモン一絞り" strj_rewrite_as_def(   "\u60e1\u3068\u5047\u9762\u306e\u30eb\u30fc\u30eb",   read_rewrite_def(system.file("def/kyuji.def", package = "audubon")) ) #> [1] "悪と仮面のルール"

title: Romanize Japanese text — strj_romanize
text: Converts Japanese kana text Latin script using selectable romanization system. function transliterates Japanese text romaji according specified convention. Non-kana characters omitted output.

title: Romanize Japanese text — strj_romanize
code: strj_romanize(   text,   config = c("wikipedia", "traditional hepburn", "modified hepburn", "kunrei", "nihon") )

title: Romanize Japanese text — strj_romanize
text: text character vector containing Japanese text. config string specifying romanization system use.

title: Romanize Japanese text — strj_romanize
text: character vector containing romanized text.

title: Romanize Japanese text — strj_romanize
text: Supported romanization systems include variants Hepburn well Kunrei-shiki Nihon-shiki conventions.

title: Tokenize Japanese text — strj_tokenize
text: Tokenizes Japanese character strings using selectable segmentation engine returns result list data frame. function provides unified interface multiple Japanese text segmentation backends. External command-based engines removed v0.6.0, tokenization performed using -process implementations. strj_segment() strj_tinyseg() aliases strj_tokenize() "budoux" "tinyseg" engines, respectively.

title: Tokenize Japanese text — strj_tokenize
code: strj_tokenize(   text,   format = c("list", "data.frame"),   engine = c("stringi", "budoux", "tinyseg"),   split = FALSE,   ... )  strj_segment(text, format = c("list", "data.frame"), split = FALSE)  strj_tinyseg(text, format = c("list", "data.frame"), split = FALSE)

title: Tokenize Japanese text — strj_tokenize
text: text character vector Japanese text tokenize. format string specifying output format. engine string specifying tokenization engine use. split logical value indicating whether text split individual sentences tokenization. ... Additional arguments passed underlying engine.

title: Tokenize Japanese text — strj_tokenize
text: format = "list", named list character vectors, one per input element. format = "data.frame", data frame containing document identifiers tokenized text.

title: Tokenize Japanese text — strj_tokenize
text: following engines supported: "stringi": Uses ICU-based boundary analysis via stringi. "budoux": Uses rule-based Japanese phrase segmentation algorithm. "tinyseg": Uses TinySegmenter-compatible statistical model. legacy "mecab" "sudachipy" engines removed v0.6.0.

what: Transcribe integers into Japanese kanji numerals — strj_transcribe_num
title: Transcribe integers into Japanese kanji numerals — strj_transcribe_num
text: Converts integer values Japanese kanji numeral representations. function transcribes integers trillions place kanji numerals. larger numbers comprehensive numeral support, consider using CRAN package arabic2kansuji.

title: Transcribe integers into Japanese kanji numerals — strj_transcribe_num
Transcribe integers into Japanese kanji numerals — strj_transcribe_num
text: int integer vector transcribe.

title: Transcribe integers into Japanese kanji numerals — strj_transcribe_num
text: character vector containing kanji numeral representations.

title: Transcribe integers into Japanese kanji numerals — strj_transcribe_num

text: CRAN release: 2025-12-21

title: audubon 0.6.1
text: Added label_wrap_jp() label_wrap_jp_gen() Japanese word wrapping ggplot2 labellers. Added label_date_jp() label_date_jp_gen() Japanese calendar date labels ggplot2. Added strj_parse_date() parse Japanese calendar date strings POSIXct values.

title: audubon 0.6.1
text: Removed mecab sudachipy engines related arguments strj_tokenize(). Removed functions overlapping provided gibasa package. Users requiring morphological analysis related features use gibasa.

title: audubon 0.6.1
text: Performed internal refactoring maintenance improvements.

title: audubon 0.5.2
text: CRAN release: 2024-04-27 Corrected probabilistic IDF calculation global_idf3. Changed behavior norm=TRUE. Cosine nomalization now performed tf_idf values RMeCab package. Added tf="itf" idf="df" options. Refactored pack performance.

title: audubon 0.5.1
text: CRAN release: 2023-05-02 Refactored tokenize_mecab tokenize_sudachipy.

title: audubon 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-04 Added bind_lr function can calculate ‘LR’ value bigrams. pack now always returns tibble, data.frame.

title: audubon 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-12-15 bind_tf_idf2 can calculate bind term frequency, inverse document frequency, tf-idf tidy text dataset. collapse_tokens, mute_tokens, lexical_density can used handling tidy text dataset tokens. strj_tokenize now preserves original order text names. prettify now can get delim argument.

title: audubon 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-22 strj_fill_iter_mark now replaces sequence iteration marks recursively. strj_tokenize now can retrieve engine argument switch tokenizers splitting text tokens.

title: audubon 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-24 Updated ngram_tokenizer function. Added wrapper function ‘TinySegmenter’ written Taku Kudo.

title: audubon 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2022-04-02 Switched arguments order pack function. pack now accepts pull second argument n third argument. pull now can accept symbol.

title: audubon 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-02-14 Updated documentation.

title: audubon 0.1.0
text: Relicensed Apache License, Version 2.0. Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
